title: myricaceae
intro: The myricaceae rank is a family of flowering plants, commonly known as the bayberry or wax myrtle family. It consists of approximately 35 to 40 species of shrubs and small trees, primarily found in North America, Europe, and Asia. Members of this family are known for their fragrant foliage, small white or yellowish flowers, and waxy berries that are often used in perfumes and fragrances. The myricaceae family is diverse and includes several economically important species, such as the bayberry (Morella pensylvanica) and the wax myrtle (Myrica cerifera).
